item.title: Fossil kept in drawer for 40 years is Antarctica’s first dinosaur bone
item.description: A bone found in Antarctica in 1985, and kept in a drawer in England for almost four decades, turns out to belong to one of the largest types of dinosaurs to ever walk the Earth.

item.title: Brisbane tree protester taken down with cherry picker at unsanctioned Kurilpa Common community garden
item.description: Emergency services had to remove a power line to lift the woman down from the tree at Kurilpa Common, a state-owned lot taken over by "guerilla gardeners" during COVID-19.

item.title: Ahead of his Origin recall, can Bradman Best be New South Wales's saviour again?
item.description: In just two matches Bradman Best has earned a reputation as a State of Origin saviour and New South Wales will be desperate for a repeat of those heroics in the decider.

item.title: Five tonnes of antibiotic florfenicol used last summer to treat disease, Tasmanian salmon industry says
item.description: More than five tonnes of antibiotics were used to treat a disease that killed 9,000 tonnes of fish last summer, Salmon Tasmania says.

item.title: SA coroner finds 'no genuine opportunity' to prevent double-fatal Urrbrae crash
item.description: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

item.title: Two deals show Donald Trump's different approaches to key players in Middle East conflicts
item.description: Two recent Middle East deals, one in Iran and the other in Lebanon, seem at odds with each other, and show how the Trump administration is running different approaches depending on who it is talking to at any given moment.

item.title: Whyalla steelworks jobs at risk if 'one last crack' to restart blast furnace fails
item.description: The aging blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks could be permanently shut down before the steelworks transitions to a direct-reduced iron plant and electric arc furnace, placing hundreds of jobs at risk.

item.title: Sunraysia Football Netball League adopts new concussion app to improve transparency
item.description: A sporting league in regional Victoria is the first to adopt a concussion app aimed at improving communication and accountability around concussions at grassroots sports.

item.title: TGA warns several sexual enhancement supplements contain hidden prescription drugs
item.description: The TGA issues a warning for consumers using sexual enhancement products after an investigation finds several products contain undeclared prescription drugs that could cause serious side effects.

item.title: Anger in Far North Queensland after Hill electorate abolished in redistribution
item.description: There is widespread anger among voters of an abolished Queensland electorate who will potentially face an 11-hour drive to their local MP's office.

item.title: Melbourne childcare provider stripped of federal funding over serious safety breaches
item.description: The Melbourne childcare provider has been cut off under new powers that allow the federal government to withdraw funding due to safety breaches.

item.title: What critics are saying about Legally Blonde prequel Elle
item.description: Legally Blonde was an overwhelming hit, so what do critics think of the prequel Elle?

item.title: Menswear brand accused of using AI to 'whitewash' model’s image
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scientists baffled at 60 per cent drop in migrating whale sightings off South West of WA
item.description: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.

item.title: Avalon Airshow organisers force pilots higher into air after crash investigation findings
item.description: A report from the Australian Transport Safety Bureau into a crash at the Avalon Airshow last year will result in changes to how low stunt pilots can fly at the event.

item.title: June sees biggest monthly fall in national housing values since 2022, Cotality finds
item.description: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

item.title: Mexico seals first World Cup knockout win in 40 years
item.description: Mexico keeps its World Cup dream alive at the Azteca as Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland score to help France and Norway advance.

item.title: Small NDIS providers and families on edge as registration 'cyclone' looms
item.description: Uncertainty and confusion are sweeping across the NDIS provider sector with mandatory registration for two categories of providers starting today.

item.title: July 1 brings new rules, a tax cut and an increase to paid parental leave
item.description: A bunch of new rules come into effect today. Find out how they could impact your finances.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 1: Do you have what it takes today?
item.description: Where would you find the iconic Forrest Gump bench? And do you know where this bridge is?

item.title: Coalition focuses fire on Labor, not One Nation, in daily talking points
item.description: Coalition talking points, obtained by the ABC, make almost no direct mention of One Nation despite the minor party surging in the polls.

item.title: World Cup: Ajdin Hrustic says 'ask the boss' about Socceroos penalty preparations
item.description: The Socceroos know their knockout game against Egypt could come down to penalties, but they aren't revealing anything about their strategies.

item.title: Conversion practice ban legislation set to be debated in Tasmanian parliament
item.description: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

item.title: Relief for home owners after Sunshine Coast properties spared from resumption
item.description: Dozens of homes on the Sunshine Coast have been spared after the Queensland government decided they were no longer required for a massive new public transport project.

item.title: Life-changing HIV medication made free in SA in step welcomed by advocates
item.description: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

item.title: New research reveals Queensland's skin cancer burden, triggers calls for more prevention campaigns
item.description: Middle-aged Queenslanders undergo almost 1.5 million skin cancer procedures a year, according to one of the studies.

item.title: Beechworth doctors revert to home visits for patients after taxi service left town
item.description: When their local taxi service folded 18 months ago, Beechworth residents struggled to get to their medical appointments.

item.title: NT teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible despite crackdown
item.description: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

item.title: Australia hammers West Indies to claim a spot in the Women's T20 World Cup cricket final
item.description: Australia makes it through to another Women's T20 World Cup final, after a scary moment when West Indies star Deandra Dottin collapsed during the national anthems.

item.title: Social housing tenants unable to work more for fear of losing homes
item.description: Fremantle resident Trish Owen could lose the tenancy on her government-provided home if she works more hours — and a new study suggests many people are in the same situation.

item.title: Alex de Minaur through to Wimbledon second round after tough day for Australian men
item.description: Alex de Minaur makes a strong start to Wimbledon in a first round that saw carnage for his Australian counterparts.

item.title: Queensland farmers oppose proposed sandstone quarry on family farm
item.description: A young Queensland farmer says his family's property is under threat from a proposed sandstone quarry, but the company behind the project insists there is nothing to fear.

item.title: Victim-survivors of alleged sexual abuse speak out on legal impact of receiving EMDR therapy
item.description: Victim-survivors of alleged sexual abuse have spoken to the ABC of the tension between EMDR mental health therapy and seeking justice through the legal system.

item.title: National Trust of South Australia volunteers formally warned against speaking publicly
item.description: Volunteers who spoke to the media say they felt "threatened" with formal warnings from the National Trust of South Australia. They are now preparing to share their experiences in a parliamentary inquiry.

item.title: Heartbreaking loss prompts conversations about farmers' mental health in Talimba, NSW
item.description: Leanne Staniforth says her husband, John, suffered in silence before his death by suicide. She is raising her voice to break down the stigma around depression.

item.title: Power, water and sewerage prices across the NT increase by 5.3 per cent from today
item.description: The Northern Territory treasurer has defended an increase in power, water and sewerage prices, which are set to rise by 5.3 per cent from today.

item.title: New laws for WA tow truck operators come into effect today
item.description: The new laws come into effect from today, including "fit and proper person" tests for business operators.

item.title: Victorian Liberals can't keep on track even with a prize in sight
item.description: The Liberals, it seems, can't resist returning to damaging infighting, even in the one part of the country where things have been going quite encouragingly for them.

item.title: Rural disability advocate wheels 440km to raise money for mental health support
item.description: Sam Longmore is wheeling across the Riverina in the rain to raise money for mental health services.

item.title: World Cup: French unity on display against Sweden as their ruthless run to title rolls on
item.description: In a French team littered with superstars it is something intangible that could stop them taking another World Cup title, following another clinical display against Sweden in the round of 32.

item.title: Liberal Party to meet to determine Moira Deeming's future after assault allegation
item.description: Opposition Leader Jess Wilson says the state executive of the Liberal Party will meet to determine Moira Deeming's candidacy for the November election.

item.title: Vanuatu deal a sign of what's to come in 'permanent contest' for Pacific influence with China
item.description: Although a big moment in diplomacy, the Vanuatu agreement has come at a clear reputational cost to Australia.

item.title: Who is Maya Joint, the Australian player who beat seven-time champion Serena Williams at Wimbledon?
item.description: Maya Joint was born in April 2006 when Serena Williams had already won seven Grand Slam singles titles. So who is the 20-year-old who just defeated a tennis legend in her return to the All England Club?

item.title: Mother of murdered Toyah Cordingley gives rare interview to thank police officers after award win
item.description: Eight years on from her daughter's brutal murder, Vanessa Gardiner says nominating the investigating offices for an award was one of the few ways she could express her gratitude.

item.title: New seafood country-of-origin labelling in Australian hospitality venues
item.description: New labelling laws for hospitality venues serving seafood should help diners know exactly what they are eating.

item.title: Is anyone happy with Australia's housing market? A new film asks the question
item.description: If you've been wondering how and why Australian house prices spun out of control you're not alone. One filmmaker was so depressed by it he went out and shot a documentary.

item.title: Chinese ambassador and ASIO trade barbs over foreign interference allegations
item.description: China's ambassador to Australia attacks ASIO over allegations of foreign interference aired in a video played at an agency event.

item.title: Taiwanese national charged over alleged people smuggling operation in Far North Queensland
item.description: The group allegedly came ashore from a boat at Pennefather Beach in the early hours of yesterday Tuesday morning.

item.title: SA Police film electric motorcycle rider doing wheelie while attending another crash
item.description: SA Police publishes a video of a teenager on an electric motorcycle doing a wheelie and then crashing into a car while they were attending another collision at the same location in Adelaide's southern suburbs.

item.title: More rain to follow overnight storm in parts of South Australia
item.description: Wet and wild weather is lashing parts of South Australia with the Bureau of Meteorology warning about wind risk during storms.

item.title: Perrottet brothers, councillors and prohibited donors to face ICAC corruption hearings
item.description: Allegations against a former NSW premier's brothers, a fugitive developer and Sydney councillors are the centre of ICAC hearings.

item.title: How Thailand's Pattaya City tried to shed its sex tourism hub reputation
item.description: It has become the centre of international attention after an Australian allegedly murdered a teenage girl, but Pattaya City has had an international reputation for decades.

item.title: Trump has revealed his financial statement. Here's how much he earned
item.description: Donald Trump's financial disclosure included $US370,000 in income from gifts and $US80 million from settlements tied to lawsuits.

item.title: Neil the seal's celebrity status putting him at risk, authorities warn
item.description: Neil the seal has become one of Tasmania's biggest celebrities. But authorities are concerned his popularity could put him and the public at risk.

item.title: Urban Utilities backs down on planned price hike after criticism from Queensland government
item.description: A major south-east Queensland water retailer has backed down on a planned price hike, which left the government's key cost-of-living measure in tatters.

item.title: Moore Park Golf Course carve up makes way for new green space in inner Sydney
item.description: The plan attracted controversy from the golfing community as the NSW government struggles to find more green spaces.

item.title: Designer featured on Gardening Australia given temporary NDIS relief after funding stripped
item.description: Garden designer Joshua Ruff has been given a six-month reprieve by the National Disability Insurance Agency (NDIA) after speaking out about changes to his NDIS plan.

item.title: What the Supreme Court ruling means for Trump's bid to restrict birthright citizenship
item.description: While it's a blow to the US president, the highest court's narrow ruling may have emboldened efforts to overturn birthright citizenship through other avenues.

item.title: Swans CEO Matthew Pavlich reflects on playing in Sydney's first little-known Pride game
item.description: This Friday will mark 10 years of Sydney Swans Pride games for premiership points.

item.title: Free meningococcal B vaccine available to Tasmanian infants under $4m program
item.description: Vaccinations for the rare but potentially deadly meningococcal B disease are now free for all Tasmanian infants for the next four years.

item.title: Coalition labels review of 2025 election a 'hyper-partisan witch-hunt'
item.description: A Coalition group has launched an extraordinary attack on one of parliament's top committees, slamming its election inquiry as biased.

item.title: Former ACM executive Antony Catalano's case adjourned till October
item.description: The former Australian Community Media executive is accused of assaulting his wife at their St Kilda penthouse in March.

item.title: Cash payments for Wagga Wagga businesses involved in alleged fraudulent visa scheme
item.description: Wagga Wagga businessman Alan Eldridge tells the NSW District Court he received a $30,000 payment for employing a Chinese national through an initiative introduced to him by former NSW MP Daryl Maguire.

item.title: Johnathan Paul Parker denies murdering Krystal Marshall, but admits burning body, court hears
item.description: Johnathan Paul Parker allegedly told a friend that he had strangled his housemate, Krystal Marshall, and asked for help to dispose of her body, a court has heard.

item.title: James Hird confirms spot in Essendon AFL coaching race
item.description: James Hird is officially a contender for Essendon's senior coaching position, as the club continues the process of finding a permanent replacement for Brad Scott.

item.title: Prominent tech entrepreneur and former minister sentenced to jail in Indonesian corruption case
item.description: Indonesian tech entrepreneur and former education minister Nadiem Makarim has been sentenced to 10 years' jail in a corruption case linked to the procurement of Google Chromebooks.

item.title: Devastated families call for more police resources to investigate cold cases
item.description: An inquiry into missing people and unsolved murders has heard from families frustrated that police can't follow up on new leads quickly.

item.title: ARN Media set to dump Karl Stefanovic from radio show, presenter defends controversial interview
item.description: Karl Stefanovic will be dumped from his new radio show amid fallout from his interview with Tommy Robinson, the ABC understands.

item.title: Village People founding member, lead singer Victor Willis dead at 74
item.description: The lead singer and a founding member of the Village People disco group has died at the age of 74.

item.title: Wednesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Department of Health issues alert after heroin found in methamphetamine and cocaine across Victoria
item.description: One person has died and multiple people have overdosed across Victoria, leading to the discovery of heroin in substances thought to be methamphetamine or cocaine.

item.title: Two people dead after collision in Victoria's south-west
item.description: Two people have died after a collision near an intersection about 30 kilometres north of Colac, in Victoria's south-west.

item.title: Police who handcuffed UK student Henry Nowak before he died being investigated for gross misconduct
item.description: Student Henry Nowak, 18, died while handcuffed by police in the English city of Southampton, after calling out "I can't breathe" and "I've been stabbed".

item.title: ‘Progressive patriotism’ central to Pat Conroy's AUKUS pitch to Labor faithful
item.description: Defence Industry Minister Pat Conroy is set to appeal to the Labor faithful to get behind AUKUS as he announces a defence shake-up targeting wasteful spending.

item.title: Canada will join the Eurovision Song Contest semi-finals in Bulgaria
item.description: While Canadian Céline Dion gave Switzerland its second Eurovision victory in 1988, next year will be the first time Canada competes in the song contest.

item.title: USA's World Cup dream alive despite red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2.

item.title: Cheat Sheet: How Jackass delighted teens and terrified parents
item.description: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

item.title: Albanese and Chalmers see reform and risk-taking as an antidote to populism
item.description: The slight recovery in Labor's polling shifts assumptions about what's in store for the remainder of this parliamentary term.

item.title: Heading the ball is integral to football but concerns about its risks are growing
item.description: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

item.title: Tunchanok Donhomla's family lays Thai teen's ashes to rest and demands death penalty for alleged murder
item.description: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by an Australian man has placed her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

item.title: Influencers to be targeted in Albanese government's gambling advertising crackdown
item.description: The Albanese government will target social media influencers and individuals who promote gambling on social media as part of its proposed laws restricting advertising.

item.title: Queensland's first koala egg and sperm bank created to preserve genetic diversity
item.description: Scientists hope the biobank will help reduce chlamydia infections in future koala populations, and act as a safety net for the survival of the endangered Australian icon.

item.title: Homicide detectives investigate death of teenager outside Craigieburn medical centre
item.description: A teenage boy stabbed to death outside a medical centre in Melbourne's north last night has been remembered as a loyal and funny friend.

item.title: Government looks at encouraging turning empty shops into regional housing
item.description: As businesses shut up shop in regional main streets, the government is weighing up whether they could help solve the housing crisis.

item.title: Murray River floodplain restoration project challenged by protesters and traditional owner
item.description: A protest camp on the banks of the Murray River enters its sixth week as traditional owners and locals fight a plan to return water to nine flood plains they say will kill trees and wildlife.

item.title: NSW Police sergeant unlawfully accessed internal database for friend accused of domestic violence
item.description: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

item.title: World Cup hero Harry Kane leads England to final 16 with 2-1 victory over DR Congo
item.description: Facing World Cup elimination at the hands of DR Congo, England needed a hero to step up when it mattered most. Harry Kane was that man.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina sets up Wimbledon clash with Naomi Osaka, Mirra Andreeva loses to Barbora Krejčíková
item.description: Ahead of their third-round Wimbledon clash, Australian Daria Kasatkina is heartened to hear Naomi Osaka has, like her, never been past the third round at the All England Club.

item.title: Wimbledon matches briefly interrupted as England fans watch World Cup on phones
item.description: England fans watch the football on their phones during Wimbledon, interrupting play briefly to cheer on Harry Kane.

item.title: Alice Springs drug and alcohol rehab reduced to one-third of size due to flooding
item.description: Drug and Alcohol Services Australia is running programs at a vastly reduced capacity due to flooding at its Alice Springs site earlier this year.

item.title: Novak Djokovic beats Stefanos Tsitsipas at Wimbledon, pranking ball kid along the way
item.description: A relaxed Novak Djokovic takes time out between sets of his second-round Wimbledon win over Stefanos Tsitsipas to play a prank on a ball kid.

item.title: Wheatbelt farmers warn dingoes will 'wreak havoc' on sheep after dog culls stopped
item.description: Farmers voice concern for their flocks after wild dog control is halted on jointly managed conservation land in parts of the Wheatbelt.

item.title: Namadgi National Park shows signs of climate change as rangers intervene
item.description: Like melting glaciers in Antarctica or bleached coral on the Great Barrier Reef, Namadgi National Park is showing signs of damage linked to global warming.

item.title: Nick Kyrgios bows out of likely last Wimbledon in doubles loss with Alexander Bublik
item.description: Australian Nick Kyrgios says he has almost certainly played his final Wimbledon match after losing in the first round of the men's doubles alongside Alexander Bublik.

item.title: Drone baiting on remote WA island shows early signs of mice eradication
item.description: A world-first drone-baiting program to protect migratory bird populations shows early signs of success off Western Australia's north coast.

item.title: Lethal Weapon actor Danny Glover says he has Alzheimer's
item.description: Danny Glover, who turns 80 in July, has revealed that he has Alzheimer's disease and is leaning on his family for support as he lives with the incurable condition.

item.title: Flooding damages nesting sites for endangered white-throated snapping turtles
item.description: An environmental group says steep banks, loose sand and low water levels are resulting in extremely low clutch numbers.

item.title: Great Koala National Park blamed as NSW forestry jobs cut in Coffs Harbour
item.description: Concerns about the sustainability of the New South Wales timber industry are growing as work continues to establish the Great Koala National Park.

item.title: Facing public backlash in Tasmania, Firmus moves to calm concerns about its AI data centres
item.description: One of three AI factories planned for northern Tasmania would be quieter than a library to the nearest resident, Firmus co-CEO Oliver Curtis says, as the company moves to assuage community concerns around power use, water and noise.

item.title: Four boys arrested in Rundle Mall, including 12yo allegedly with machete
item.description: A 12-year-old boy who allegedly had a machete concealed in his pants was among four boys arrested in Rundle Mall over two days.

item.title: Ben Te Kura leaves Brisbane Broncos for NFL dream as Corey Jensen faces uncertain future
item.description: The Brisbane Broncos are releasing forward Ben Te Kura from the rest of his NRL contract to allow him to pursue his dream of playing in the NFL.

item.title: Financial misconduct compensation scheme faces $170 million cost blowout
item.description: The First Guardian and Shield super disasters, and Dixon Advisory fallout, is causing a cost blowout in the taxpayer-funded scheme that is designed to compensate victims of financial misconduct.

item.title: Christian Brothers granted pause on child abuse victim payouts due to financial strain
item.description: The Christian Brothers religious order has been granted a moratorium on all civil claims against it, after arguing it was running out of money to pay victims of child sexual abuse.

item.title: Mile Jedinak knows what it's like to take a World Cup penalty and he has advice for the Socceroos
item.description: Now an assistant coach for the Socceroos, and with a penalty shootout a possibility when Australia face Egypt in the round of 32, Mile Jedinak knows what it takes to seize the moment.

item.title: Man dies after car hits fallen tree following severe storms near Cowra
item.description: Wind gusts of about 100 kilometres an hour were recorded as severe storms hit the NSW Central West overnight with tragic consequences.

item.title: Suburban backyards in Perth to be overhauled in major R-code subdivision changes
item.description: Major planning changes will allow more Perth home owners to subdivide their properties in a bid to boost housing supply as the city continues to fall short of its infill target.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Feuding Belgians save the day, England's claims of 'disadvantage'
item.description: Belgium stars turn on each other before combining to save their team, England makes claims of "disadvantage", and DR Congo's goalkeeper gives it all he's got. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Firmus CEOs talk about energy use for planned AI factories
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sunshine Coast man charged over alleged offshore child abuse offences
item.description: A Sunshine Coast man has faced court today after being charged by the AFP with child abuse offences, including the alleged live online sexual abuse of children in the Philippines.

item.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help search for Venezuela earthquake survivors
item.description: A week after devastating twin earthquakes struck Venezuela rescue teams are using drones, dogs, cameras and specialised listening equipment to search for survivors.

item.title: Wallabies fullback Jock Campbell earns Test recall to face Ireland in Nations Championship opener
item.description: Jock Campbell emerges from almost four years in the international rugby wilderness to snare the prized fullback role for the Wallabies in their Nations Championship opener against Ireland.

item.title: Adelaide gets 70 per cent of July rain in one day with windy weather set to continue
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology says metropolitan Adelaide has received 70 per cent of its median rainfall for July in a single day, as South Australian farmers welcome the conditions.

item.title: Oxford Hotel arsonist Joseph Chehade jailed over $1.5 million North Adelaide blaze
item.description: A judge has jailed a man who "embarked on a consistent campaign of terror" against his former business partner, including torching the complex containing the Oxford Hotel in North Adelaide.

item.title: $160 million government loan aims to protect an outback fertiliser industry
item.description: Ryowa Group has received a loan for its outback Queensland Phosphate Hill asset as the federal and state governments move to ramp up domestic fertiliser production capability.

item.title: Queensland government defends fundraiser charging up to $10,500 for 15-minute meetings with ministers
item.description: The LNP’s so-called Corporate Observers Program allowed donors to meet with ministers in allocated 15-minute appointments.

item.title: Victorian government to review puppy farm laws amid 'loophole' concerns
item.description: The Victorian government will review animal welfare laws to tighten loopholes being "exploited" by puppy farmers.

item.title: Are parents reconsidering letting kids play footy due to CTE risks?
item.description: How is the conversation about lasting brain disease from repeated head knocks on the sporting field impacting parents' decisions about what sports their children play?

item.title: Government urged to crack down on NDIS kickbacks and bad actors as fraud inquiry handed down
item.description: A Labor-led parliamentary inquiry into fraud in the NDIS has made 12 recommendations to improve the integrity of the scheme.

item.title: Australia ranks third-highest for median wealth globally, UBS data shows
item.description: Australia holds the third-highest median wealth globally at $306,074, pipped at the post by Luxembourg and Belgium.

item.title: Former St Mary's Hostel in Alice Springs heritage listed as significant Stolen Generations site
item.description: Former residents say the heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

item.title: NSW MP Helen Dalton's agricultural company fined over quad bike death
item.description: The court heard the 82-year-old worker was not wearing a helmet and the quad had no rollover protection when the crash occurred.

item.title: Russia strikes in central Kyiv kill at least 25 and injure dozens
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

item.title: Record rains fuel buffel weed surge prompting calls for stronger SA management
item.description: As buffel grass explodes across Central Australia due to high rainfall, there are fears it will spread further east and threaten landmarks in South Australia and other states.

item.title: Catholic Schools NSW CEO Dallas McInerney to stand aside amid ICAC probe
item.description: The head of the body governing the state's catholic schools will stand aside after he was named in a corruption probe investigating donations to the NSW Liberal Party.

item.title: Dean Solomon yet to make decision on Bombers AFL coach role after James Hird confirms candidacy
item.description: James Hird's open ambitions have done little to sway Dean Solomon, with the interim Essendon coach yet to declare his future plans.

item.title: Look inside US President Donald Trump's new $580m Air Force One jet
item.description: The jet, gifted to the US government by Qatar, has been retrofitted, with Trump shaking off concerns over the cost.

item.title: WA Police defend doubling in strip searches amid rise in arrests
item.description: The number of people strip searched by police officers in Western Australia more than doubles in three years, leading some to label the practice a human rights violation.

item.title: Adelaide Thunderbirds fans will be in full cry at Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

item.title: Ex-Flinders Island school principal Gary Raymond Sykes jailed for child sex abuse
item.description: A retired Tasmanian school principal has been sentenced to four years in prison for child sex abuse.

item.title: Former Sydney childcare worker David James jailed for up to 12 years over child abuse offences
item.description: A painstaking victim identification process was undertaken by Australian Federal Police to establish his offending occurred at six of out-of-school hours care centres over a three-year period.

item.title: Prices rise at Alice Springs cattle sale, with steers fetching more than $6/kg
item.description: The strong results at the Alice Springs sale reflect the overall Australian cattle market, which has surged in recent weeks.

item.title: School worker indecently assaulted girls in Adelaide Hills, court told
item.description: A contract worker accused of indecently assaulted two students at an Adelaide Hills school he worked at has appeared in court for the first time.

item.title: Visa fees increase without warning, sparking outrage and disappointment
item.description: International students and families say they feel shocked and exploited after visa fees across dozens of categories rose on July 1 without warning, with some surging more than 200 per cent.

item.title: Teen accused of abducting 17yo girl near Melbourne school faces court
item.description: A teen has been accused of abducting a 17-year-old girl from near a Melbourne school with the intention of forcing her to marry him.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Lions hold on for win over Geelong in Kardinia classic
item.description: Brisbane Lions earn a second straight win over Geelong at Kardinia Park in a high-scoring epic, as the Lions jump into the top four.

item.title: John Dewar accepts UOW appointment would benefit KordaMentha, ICAC hears
item.description: ICAC hears that John Dewar wanted his UOW appointment to benefit KordaMentha and his acknowledgement there could be a perception of a conflict of interest.

item.title: Nine Thai monks killed after boy drives truck into procession in Mukdahan province
item.description: An 11-year-old has driven his parents' truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing nine monks.

item.title: New South Wales government cracks down on sales of potentially deadly nitrous oxide
item.description: After a 7.30 investigation exposed the increasing dangers of nitrous oxide use, NSW has announced reforms will come into place shortly.

item.title: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy says anti-gambling changes contain streaming 'loophole'
item.description: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy argues the federal government's proposed changes to gambling advertising laws contain a streaming loophole.

item.title: De Minaur cruises through to Wimbledon third round with straight-sets win
item.description: Australian Alex de Minaur has raced into the third round at Wimbledon with a straight-sets victory over tricky French veteran Adrian Mannarino.

item.title: Maggie O'Farrell sets her latest novel, Land, against the backdrop of the Great Famine in Ireland
item.description: The Academy Award-nominated writer dives into Irish history in Land, revealing how the Great Famine changed the country forever.

item.title: The best seasonal produce to look for in July
item.description: Here are some of the best fruit and vegetables to look for as we head further into winter.

item.title: Iran war proves why Australia's last two refineries still matter, Ampol boss says
item.description: Ampol's chief executive says the Iran conflict exposed Australia's fuel vulnerability and strengthened the case for keeping its remaining refinery operating.

item.title: Joanna Howe and Nat Cook clash at pre-poll booth
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: When is Taylor Swift getting married? Clues point to a weekend wedding in the heart of New York City
item.description: Fans believe they know the when and where of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's upcoming nuptials, but much of the reported two-day extravaganza will likely be a cloak-and-dagger operation.

item.title: How World Cup watch parties in pubs and cafes became Australia's new favourite hobby
item.description: They were just supposed to be somewhere to watch the match, but World Cup viewing parties have ended up becoming so much more.

item.title: Firmus wants to build three AI data centres in Tasmania, but can the state handle them?
item.description: An artificial intelligence company wants to build three AI factories in Tasmania, but questions are being raised about the impacts and benefits for the state.

item.title: Lachie Kennedy's Prefontaine Classic run a Commonwealth Games precursor
item.description: Lachie Kennedy is a big fish in a relatively small pond, but the next few weeks will pit him against some of the world's best as he continues his rise in global athletics.

item.title: Former US Olympian charged over alleged 'vandalism' of US Reflecting Pool
item.description: Former US Olympian David 'Davey' Hearn has been accused of "maliciously" breaking or destroying lining material on the bottom of the Washington DC pool, court documents show

item.title: Greater awareness of concussion in children's sport leaving parents 'terrified', experts say
item.description: A research paper published in June found almost a third of parents surveyed said they stopped their children from playing contact sports out of fear of concussion.

item.title: Victoria Park closure fuels international students' fears of loneliness, isolation
item.description: Foreign university students say the closure of the park to make way for Brisbane Stadium leaves them without a crucial meeting place to help stave off loneliness and isolation.

item.title: Liberal MP Moira Deeming's future set to be decided at meeting of party's state executive
item.description: Mrs Deeming has lodged a case against Victorian Liberal Party president Brian Loughnane in the Supreme Court.

item.title: Australia's healthcare system still weighted towards male experience, women's health experts say
item.description: Australia has come a long way when it comes to women's healthcare, but experts say medical misogyny is still very much alive — and it's not just attitudes that are the problem.

item.title: Blizzard finally dumps snow after Australia's warmest June in decades
item.description: Following the worst start to a ski season in decades, a major snowstorm hit the NSW and Victorian Alps last night.

item.title: Shareholders warn governments against bailouts to Trafigura, owner of Nyrstar
item.description: A group of international shareholders are warning state and federal governments against giving global commodities giant Trafigura hundreds of millions of dollars to keep smelting operations going in Australia.

item.title: Steven Dennis welcomes drug-driving law change after wife's death
item.description: A man devastated by a drug-driving "loophole" that allowed a meth-affected driver to avoid a drug conviction after a fatal crash welcomes new legislation.

item.title: Aboriginal activist Ruth Langford takes on Sustainable Timber Tasmania staff during trespass case
item.description: An Aboriginal activist has interrogated forestry company staff over their knowledge of cultural heritage as part of her defence against a trespass charge.

item.title: Captains Flat residents welcome $1 million fund to remediate lead contaminated soil on private land
item.description: Residents of Captains Flat have welcomed the promise of a $1 million fund to help them remediate lead contamination on private properties, but some fear it will not be enough.

item.title: SA government urged to tackle explosion in feral pig numbers after floods
item.description: Feral pig numbers are exploding in outback South Australia after record-breaking floods, prompting a call from station owners and the Invasive Species Council for governments to take action.

item.title: Victims 'blindsided' by moratorium on all cases against Christian Brothers, lawyers say
item.description: Lawyers argue the court's decision to grant a moratorium had thrown abuse survivors into uncertainty.

item.title: Senator speaks up for Mount Isa families in limbo as forced house auctions loom
item.description: Victorian senator Lidia Thorpe joins a fight to stop Indigenous homes being sold in Queensland due to unpaid rates.

item.title: Alex de Minaur defeats Adrian Mannarino at Wimbledon and explains his two mindsets after shaky performance
item.description: An imperfect Alex de Minaur explains his two mindsets as the Australian makes it through another round at Wimbledon.

item.title: Maya Joint falls to Alexandra Eala after her Wimbledon victory over Serena Williams
item.description: Maya Joint loses in the second round at Wimbledon, leaving just one Australian woman still in grand slam contention.

item.title: Told he was 'too young' to have cancer, Rory died a year later
item.description: Rory Esler's family and friends will take part in the Gold Coast marathon festival this weekend to honour his memory and to raise awareness, encouraging young Australians to advocate for themselves when navigating the medical system.

item.title: World Cup: Egypt leaves Mohamed Salah fitness decision for Socceroos clash until last minute
item.description: The "passionate" Egypt captain trains with the squad but his coach says he won't be rushed into the team to face Australia if he's not fit.

item.title: Community leaders back Coles supermarket rejection in Kalgoorlie-Boulder
item.description: Welcoming a landmark decision by the consumer watchdog, Kalgoorlie-Boulder's peak business group says the city's 30,000-strong population does not justify the development of another supermarket.

item.title: Knights defeat Sharks 18-4 in heated opening to NRLW season
item.description: Newcastle starts life without Jesse Southwell with a strong victory over the Sharks in the NRLW opener.

item.title: The brotherhood behind arm wrestling's grip
item.description: A growing community on the Gold Coast is giving men a place to not only build strength, but also to become brothers in arms.

item.title: Millie Bobby Brown and Louis Partridge on filming abroad and female empowerment
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: One of WA's last World War II veterans turns 104 after cheating death twice
item.description: Eighty five years ago, Brian Walley, a prisoner of war in a German camp after crashing his bomber into the North Sea, doubted he would live for long. Yesterday, he celebrated his 104th birthday.

item.title: $184.1m budget injection will not solve long waiting lists for services, DV peak body warns
item.description: A recent $184.1 million state budget investment is not enough of a "breakthrough" for critically outstretched domestic violence services, says Delia Donovan.

item.title: Elmeré van der Berg is the buy of the Super Netball season, but she almost signed for the opposing grand final team
item.description: South African import Elmeré van der Berg has scored more goals than anyone else in her debut Super Netball season. Ahead of the grand final she speaks about her 'resting bitch face', retraining her brain to embrace weight gain, and the tweaks the Adelaide Thunderbirds have made to take her game to another level.

item.title: Papua separatists kill US pilot amid claim Indonesian troops were being brought to region
item.description: A Papua separatist group has shot dead an American pilot who allegedly transported Indonesian troops into the restive province.

item.title: Tour de France, how to watch, when does it start, can Jonas Vingegaard beat Tadej Pogačar?
item.description: Tadej Pogačar, defending champion is the man to beat as the 116th Tour de France gets underway in Barcelona on Saturday. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Firefighters' union loses Supreme Court bid to keep corruption probe secret
item.description: An investigation into allegations of corruption during the tenure of former Victorian premier Daniel Andrews was finished in 2022, but its release has been delayed by court action.

item.title: Suspected bird flu cases detected in NSW and WA after devastating impact overseas
item.description: A suspected positive case of H5 bird flu has been identified in New South Wales, the state agriculture minister has announced.

item.title: Kyiv suffers deadliest attack this year, after Russia 'unleashed hell'
item.description: At least 27 people are dead and the city's key Red Cross warehouse has been destroyed, as search efforts are still underway.

item.title: Former AFL great Nicky Winmar found guilty of assaulting woman in northern Victoria
item.description: The woman previously told the court she was "fearful" for her life on the night of the incident last year.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Portugal and Croatia's instant classic, England pubs prepare for Mexico
item.description: Portugal and Croatia play one of the greatest halves of football in World Cup history, packed with legendary players, brilliant goals and more VAR drama than anyone could handle. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Tougher penalties, but no gun limit under Tasmania's proposed new firearms laws post-Bondi
item.description: New gun laws, which create tougher penalties for the theft and possession of stolen firearms, but do not include gun caps, have been unveiled by the Tasmanian government, asking for public feedback.

item.title: Christopher Mark Jordan trial for Shyanne Lee-Tatnell's 2023 death likely in April next year
item.description: A man who has pleaded not guilty to murdering 14-year-old Tasmanian teenager Shyanne Lee-Tatnell in 2023 will likely stand trial early next year.

item.title: Cause of wrongly-positioned aircraft control in Shellharbour fatal crash unknown
item.description: Investigators are unable to work out why a take-off control was wrongly-positioned leading to a fatal plane crash at Shellharbour Airport last October.

item.title: Chien-Wen Peng granted bail over allegations he helped smuggle foreign nationals to Cape York Peninsula
item.description: One of the men charged over an alleged plot to smuggle foreign nationals into Queensland by boat has told his lawyer he was just in the area to go fishing, a court has heard. The 34-year-old has been granted bail today.

item.title: Adelaide Park Lands tree activist Mij Tanith will not rule out further action despite court warning
item.description: Activist and author Mij Tanith refuses to rule out being arrested again for protesting against the felling of trees in the Adelaide Park Lands, despite a warning from a magistrate she could face a conviction next time.

item.title: West Coast Eagles star's 'grieving best mate' pens open letter to AFL leaders calling on them to act on CTE
item.description: In a five-page letter to the executives and commissioners of the AFL, a grieving best mate has slammed the organisation for "failing" former West Coasts player Adam Hunter, who suffered from CTE.

item.title: Independent MP David O'Byrne on Tasmanian government's 'messy' six months
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Craigieburn stabbing victim's alleged murderer was out on bail
item.description: Darweish Mohamed, 15, was found with stab wounds outside a hospital in Melbourne's northern suburbs on Wednesday night and died at the scene.

item.title: Police investigate shooting deaths of two horses in Kosciuszko National Park
item.description: A police investigation is underway after the discovery of two wild horse carcasses in Kosciuszko National Park.

item.title: Peabody Energy fined after worker trapped inside bulldozer in coal void
item.description: The bulldozer was trapped inside a coal valve at a mine site in Central Queensland for eight hours. The mine's owner has now been fined more than $130,000.

item.title: Emergency services warn drivers against complacency as WA road death toll climbs
item.description: With fatalities on WA roads on track to surpass their highest toll in almost two decades, first responders fear the upcoming school holidays will make things worse.

item.title: CCTV of arson attack in Malvern.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Arson attacks and shooting linked with organised crime, SA police say
item.description: Detectives say they believe arson attacks on a car wash and another on an office block in Adelaide are linked with a shooting in the city's east in February.

item.title: Pilot who crashed into Beijing's tallest building wrote about 'ending his life', Chinese authorities say
item.description: Chinese authorities say the cause of the crash was "personal reasons" but internet users still have questions.

item.title: Romen Dixon granted bail to live with parents after alleged stabbing in Toowoomba Woolworths
item.description: The parents of an 18-year-old charged after allegedly stabbing a man inside a busy regional supermarket are urging families to be more aware of the mental health of young men.

item.title: Charges dropped against two teens who allegedly abducted 17yo girl outside school
item.description: Victoria Police have dropped charges against two males who were arrested and charged with the abduction of a teenage girl outside a Melbourne school.

item.title: NSW Liberal Damien Tudehope quits the frontbench ahead of appearing at ICAC as a witness
item.description: Veteran NSW Liberal Damien Tudehope has quit the frontbench after being called as a witness in a looming corruption watchdog probe.

item.title: DarkLab ditches Transformer artwork in Huon Valley due to escalating costs
item.description: The group behind Tasmania's Dark Mofo festival has scrapped plans to develop a "destination public artwork" in the state's south-east due to escalating costs.

item.title: Australia raises concerns as China strengthens control over maritime traffic east of Taiwan
item.description: Australia raises concerns about China's latest activities near Taiwan "directly to Chinese officials" as tensions simmer in the region.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.
